tcTopicTitle: Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

topicPath.title: Navigation and Communication Systems
Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

stem: An aircraft VHF communication transmitter appears to radiate normally on a nearby handheld receiver, but has significantly reduced range to ground stations. Which is the MOST likely radio-wave related cause?

options.text: The transmitter carrier frequency is too low to propagate in the atmosphere
Excessive attenuation due to high SWR from an antenna or feedline mismatch
The receiver squelch threshold is set too high
Multipath reflection has increased the transmitted power output

explanation.correct: A high standing wave ratio (SWR) indicates poor matching between transmitter, feedline, and antenna, causing reflected power and reduced effective radiated power, which reduces range.

whyOthersAreWrong.A: VHF propagation issues are not caused by being too low within the VHF band in this context.
whyOthersAreWrong.C: Squelch affects reception audio gating, not transmitted range.
whyOthersAreWrong.D: Multipath can cause fading or distortion, not increased transmitter power output.

tcTopicTitle: Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

topicPath.title: Navigation and Communication Systems
Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

stem: A navigation receiver intermittently loses signal when the aircraft banks, with no faults found in the receiver. Which radio-wave behaviour best explains the symptom?

options.text: Refraction increases with bank angle and blocks the signal
Antenna polarization and line-of-sight geometry can reduce received field strength during manoeuvres
The signal changes from continuous wave to pulsed due to bank angle
The frequency of the received signal increases due to centrifugal force

explanation.correct: Banking can change antenna orientation relative to the transmitting station, affecting polarization alignment and line-of-sight geometry, reducing received signal strength.

whyOthersAreWrong.A: Refraction is primarily an environmental propagation effect and is not driven by bank angle in this way.
whyOthersAreWrong.C: Modulation type does not change because the aircraft banks.
whyOthersAreWrong.D: Centrifugal force does not change RF frequency; Doppler effects relate to relative velocity.

tcTopicTitle: Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

topicPath.title: Navigation and Communication Systems
Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

stem: During troubleshooting, a technician measures a normal RF output at the transmitter test port, but the antenna output is low. Which component is MOST likely introducing signal loss?

options.text: Coaxial feedline and its connectors
Microphone audio circuit
Receiver local oscillator
Aircraft intercom system

explanation.correct: Loss between the transmitter and antenna is commonly caused by coaxial cable degradation, water ingress, poor bonding, damaged connectors, or corrosion, resulting in attenuation and poor radiation.

whyOthersAreWrong.B: Microphone audio affects modulation quality, not RF power delivered to the antenna.
whyOthersAreWrong.C: The receiver local oscillator does not affect the transmitter RF power path.
whyOthersAreWrong.D: The intercom affects audio routing, not RF output to the antenna.

tcTopicTitle: Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

topicPath.title: Navigation and Communication Systems
Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

stem: A technician observes strong interference on a VHF navigation receiver whenever an onboard electrical motor operates. Which statement best describes how the interference reaches the receiver?

options.text: The motor changes the receiver’s tuned frequency directly
The motor generates broadband electromagnetic noise that can be radiated or conducted into RF paths
The motor causes the antenna to become resonant at a different harmonic
The interference is caused only by static electricity on the airframe

explanation.correct: Motors and switching devices can produce broadband EMI that couples into wiring or radiates into antennas and receiver front ends, degrading signal-to-noise ratio.

whyOthersAreWrong.A: Interference does not retune the receiver; it masks desired signals.
whyOthersAreWrong.C: Motor operation typically introduces EMI rather than changing antenna resonance.
whyOthersAreWrong.D: Static can cause noise, but the correlation with motor operation points to EMI.

tcTopicTitle: Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

topicPath.title: Navigation and Communication Systems
Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

stem: A receiver shows correct frequency selection and audio output, but weak-signal performance is poor. Which RF principle best explains why a degraded front-end amplifier would cause this?

options.text: Reduced selectivity increases the antenna gain
Increased noise figure reduces receiver sensitivity and degrades signal-to-noise ratio
Increased modulation index prevents demodulation
Reduced wavelength prevents antenna coupling

explanation.correct: A degraded RF front end increases the receiver noise figure, reducing sensitivity so weak signals are lost in noise.

whyOthersAreWrong.A: Selectivity relates to filtering, not antenna gain.
whyOthersAreWrong.C: Modulation index is a transmitter parameter, not a receiver front-end issue.
whyOthersAreWrong.D: Wavelength is set by frequency and does not change due to amplifier condition.

tcTopicTitle: Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

topicPath.title: Navigation and Communication Systems
Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

stem: Two antennas are installed too close together on the same aircraft surface. What is the MOST likely radio-wave related effect that can cause system performance issues?

options.text: The antennas will automatically synchronize frequencies
Mutual coupling can detune antennas and increase interference between systems
The closer spacing increases line-of-sight range to ground stations
The antennas will eliminate multipath reflections

explanation.correct: Close proximity can cause mutual coupling, altering impedance and radiation patterns and increasing interference between systems.

whyOthersAreWrong.C: Antenna spacing does not increase propagation range.
whyOthersAreWrong.D: Antenna proximity does not eliminate multipath.

tcTopicTitle: Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

topicPath.title: Navigation and Communication Systems
Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

stem: A VHF communication transmitter is reported as “scratchy” and difficult to understand at a distance, while nearby reception sounds acceptable. Which is the MOST likely RF-related explanation?

options.text: Overmodulation or distortion creating excessive sidebands and reducing intelligibility at lower received signal levels
The receiver volume is set too low at the ground station
The aircraft antenna has become more directional at short range only
The transmitted wavelength has decreased due to temperature

explanation.correct: Overmodulation or distortion can sound acceptable at high signal levels but becomes unintelligible as received signal level drops.

whyOthersAreWrong.B: Ground station volume does not selectively affect intelligibility with distance.
whyOthersAreWrong.C: Antenna pattern changes are not limited to short range only.
whyOthersAreWrong.D: Wavelength is determined by frequency and is not directly affected by temperature.

tcTopicTitle: Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

topicPath.title: Navigation and Communication Systems
Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

stem: A technician is checking bonding and shielding on avionics wiring near a navigation antenna feeder. Which radio-wave concept best justifies this inspection?

options.text: Shielding improves DC resistance to prevent battery drain
Proper bonding and shielding reduce unwanted RF coupling and prevent interference with desired signals
Bonding increases antenna frequency to improve reception
Shielding eliminates the need for correct antenna placement

explanation.correct: Proper bonding and shielding control RF coupling and electromagnetic compatibility, preserving signal integrity.

whyOthersAreWrong.A: Shielding and bonding are not primarily for DC battery drain.
whyOthersAreWrong.C: Bonding does not increase antenna frequency.
whyOthersAreWrong.D: Shielding does not remove the need for correct antenna placement.

tcTopicTitle: Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

topicPath.title: Navigation and Communication Systems
Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

stem: A navigation system using line-of-sight signals shows reduced reliability at low altitude in mountainous terrain. Which radio-wave effect is MOST likely contributing?

options.text: Increased ionospheric reflection improving signal strength
Shadowing and multipath causing fading and intermittent reception
Automatic frequency shift to avoid obstacles
Higher altitude always increases attenuation

explanation.correct: Terrain can block line-of-sight and create reflections that produce multipath fading, reducing reliability.

whyOthersAreWrong.A: Ionospheric reflection is associated with HF, not line-of-sight VHF/UHF systems.
whyOthersAreWrong.C: Radios do not automatically change frequency to avoid obstacles.
whyOthersAreWrong.D: Higher altitude generally improves line-of-sight coverage.

tcTopicTitle: Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

topicPath.title: Navigation and Communication Systems
Radio waves and radio signals – Theory and application

stem: An aircraft experiences intermittent communication issues only when a second transmitter on a nearby frequency is keyed. Which radio-wave phenomenon best explains the issue?

options.text: Receiver desensitization due to strong adjacent-channel energy or intermodulation products
The first transmitter changes modulation type automatically
The antenna becomes non-conductive when two frequencies are present
The receiver converts RF energy directly into DC and shuts down

explanation.correct: A strong nearby transmission can overload receiver front-end stages, causing desensitization or intermodulation that masks the desired signal.

whyOthersAreWrong.B: Transmitters do not change modulation automatically due to another transmitter.
whyOthersAreWrong.C: Antennas remain conductive; the issue is front-end overload or coupling.
whyOthersAreWrong.D: Receivers do not normally convert RF to DC and shut down due to interference.
